frameworks.name: high-density silicon electrode array technology
frameworks.one_line: integrated CMOS probe enabling large-scale single-unit population recording

frameworks.name: large-scale neural population recording
frameworks.one_line: scaling simultaneous single-neuron recording into nonhuman primate brain

frameworks.name: systems neuroscience in nonhuman primates
frameworks.one_line: macaque as close model for human cognition and behavior

core_question: Can a high-density silicon electrode array be designed to enable large-scale simultaneous single-unit recording across superficial and deep structures in the macaque brain?
key_assumptions: macaque is a close model of human cognition and behavior
single-cell resolution at large scale is the key bottleneck
programmable channel selection on a long shank supports multi-area recording
manipulations: shank length and electrode count (4416 along 45 mm vs 2496 along 25 mm)
programmable selection of 384 active channels from the array
key_findings: fabricated Neuropixels 1.0-NHP in two long-shank versions
recorded over 3000 single neurons within a single session with one probe
recorded over 1000 neurons simultaneously using multiple probes
enabled simultaneous superficial and deep multi-area recording in macaque
interpretations: represents a significant increase in recording access and scalability versus existing primate technologies
extends high-density population recording from rodents to large-animal brains
